name: Excessive Number of nslookup from Powershell
notes:
This is rule detects excessive number of nslookup from powershell.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
SIGMA Title: Nslookup PowerShell Download Cradle - ProcessCreation
SIGMA Description: Detects suspicious powershell download cradle using nslookup. This cradle uses nslookup to extract payloads from DNS records


Confidence: LOW

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of nslookup.exe
when the event matches Parent Process Name (custom) is any of [powershell.exe or pwsh.exe]
when the event matches (LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -q=txt %' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -querytype=txt %') AQL filter query
when at least 100 events are seen with the same Process Name (custom) in 1 hour(s)
thresholds: when at least 100 events are seen with the same Process Name (custom) in 1 hour(s)
TD_list: APPLY Excessive Number of nslookup from Powershell on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of nslookup.exe
AND when the event matches Parent Process Name (custom) is any of [powershell.exe or pwsh.exe]
AND when the event matches (LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -q=txt %' OR  LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -querytype=txt %') AQL filter query
AND when at least 100 events are seen with the same Process Name (custom) in 1 hour(s)
RED_list: Event name: Excessive Number of nslookup from Powershell
Event description: Detects excessive number of nslookup from powershell.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
